overview.summary: Sliding Window turns repeated contiguous-range work into a single flowing pass across the data.
learningObjectives: Track how left and right cooperate to maintain an invariant.
Explain why shrinking restores a violated constraint.
Read window code by watching the diagram first.
overview.interviewRelevance: This pattern appears constantly in array and string interviews.

paragraphs: Think of monitoring a rolling 3-hour electricity bill, not the entire month every time. You keep one live window and update it as each new reading arrives.
Streaming analytics, fraud monitoring, and substring problems all work the same way: only the current contiguous slice matters.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: A window saves work because the next answer is almost the same as the previous one.

definition.technicalDefinition: Sliding Window is a technique for maintaining information about a contiguous range while moving that range through an array or string in linear time.

vocabulary.definition: The current contiguous range being examined.
Move the right edge to include a new element.
Move the left edge to discard an old element.

keyProperties: The left and right boundaries only move forward.
The algorithm maintains an invariant about the current range.
Each element is usually added once and removed once.

metaphor: Imagine a camera frame sliding over a long mural. Only what is inside the frame matters right now.
As the frame moves right, one item enters and one item may leave.
mentalModel.clickSentence: Do not recompute the whole range. Update only what changed at the edges.

memoryDescription: Sliding Window itself is not a data structure. It is a strategy layered on top of arrays or strings.
The state usually lives in a few scalar variables: left, right, a running summary, and sometimes a frequency map.

memoryLayout.label: left
memoryLayout.value: start index of the active window

memoryLayout.label: right
memoryLayout.value: end index of the active window

memoryLayout.label: window_sum
memoryLayout.value: cached total for the current range

steps.explanation: Initialize the window on the first cell. We have not added any value yet.
Expand the window to include index 0.
Add nums[0] = 2 to the running window sum.
Expand the window to include index 1.
Add nums[1] = 1 to the running window sum.
Expand the window to include index 2.
Add nums[2] = 5 to the running window sum.
A full window of size 3 is in place. Compare its sum against the best answer so far.
Expand the window to include index 3.
Add nums[3] = 1 to the running window sum.
The window is too large, so the constraint gauge turns red and we must shrink from the left.
Subtract nums[0] = 2 before moving left forward.
Shift left to 1. The window is valid again.
A full window of size 3 is in place. Compare its sum against the best answer so far.
Expand the window to include index 4.
Add nums[4] = 3 to the running window sum.
The window is too large, so the constraint gauge turns red and we must shrink from the left.
Subtract nums[1] = 1 before moving left forward.
Shift left to 2. The window is valid again.
A full window of size 3 is in place. Compare its sum against the best answer so far.
Expand the window to include index 5.
Add nums[5] = 2 to the running window sum.
The window is too large, so the constraint gauge turns red and we must shrink from the left.
Subtract nums[2] = 5 before moving left forward.
Shift left to 3. The window is valid again.
A full window of size 3 is in place. Compare its sum against the best answer so far.
Expand the window to include index 6.
Add nums[6] = 4 to the running window sum.
The window is too large, so the constraint gauge turns red and we must shrink from the left.
Subtract nums[3] = 1 before moving left forward.
Shift left to 4. The window is valid again.
A full window of size 3 is in place. Compare its sum against the best answer so far.
Expand the window to include index 7.
Add nums[7] = 1 to the running window sum.
The window is too large, so the constraint gauge turns red and we must shrink from the left.
Subtract nums[4] = 3 before moving left forward.
Shift left to 5. The window is valid again.
A full window of size 3 is in place. Compare its sum against the best answer so far.
Finish scanning. The maximum sum of any size-3 window is 9.

operations.name: Expand Right
operations.description: Add the incoming element so the window covers one more index.
operations.visualNote: The orange pointer advances and the highlight rectangle stretches to the right.

operations.name: Shrink Left
operations.description: Drop the oldest element when the window is too large or violates a constraint.
operations.visualNote: The green pointer advances and the highlight rectangle snaps inward from the left.

patternTemplate.whenToUse: Use Sliding Window when the answer depends on a contiguous segment and neighboring windows overlap heavily.
patternTemplate.timeComplexity: O(n) because each pointer only moves forward.
patternTemplate.spaceComplexity: O(1) to O(k) depending on extra state like a frequency map.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Given a string, find the length of the longest substring with at most K distinct characters.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: The substring must stay contiguous, and we must maintain a constraint while expanding and shrinking a live window.

examples.title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
examples.problemStatement: Find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of fixed size k.
steps: Build the first full window of size 3.
Record its sum as the current best.
Slide right by removing the leftmost element and adding the new rightmost one.
Keep updating the best sum whenever the window is valid.
examples.rationale: Each index enters and leaves the sum once.

examples.title: Smallest Subarray With Sum At Least S
examples.problemStatement: Return the minimum length of a contiguous subarray whose sum is at least the target.
steps: Expand right until the sum reaches the target.
Shrink left as aggressively as possible while preserving validity.
Record the best valid length seen.
examples.rationale: The left pointer only moves when a valid window exists.

conceptQuiz.question: What makes Sliding Window possible?
conceptQuiz.explanation: We reuse most of the previous window instead of recomputing it from scratch.

conceptQuiz.question: When should the left pointer move?
options: Only when the constraint needs repair
conceptQuiz.explanation: The left pointer exists to restore the invariant after expansion.

conceptQuiz.question: Why is the runtime often O(n)?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Each element is entered once and removed once in the common pattern.

conceptQuiz.question: What kind of problems should trigger this pattern first?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Sliding Window is about contiguous subarrays and substrings.

practice.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
practice.keyInsight: Shrink left once the target is satisfied.

practice.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
practice.keyInsight: Use a dynamic window with frequency tracking.

practice.title: Permutation in String
practice.keyInsight: Keep a fixed-size character balance window.

practice.title: Find All Anagrams in a String
Maximum Erasure Value
practice.keyInsight: Maintain uniqueness while maximizing a running sum.

practice.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
practice.keyInsight: Track the dominant character count inside the window.
